title: Product Manager, Relevance and Personalization
score_rationale: Riley's 9 years of PM experience directly in AI search, search ranking, personalization, and recommendation systems is a near-perfect domain match for Airbnb's R&P team — the Samsung and Kolon roles demonstrate hands-on ownership of exactly the systems this role requires. The one notable gap is the 10+ year experience requirement (Riley has 9) and the role likely targets a Staff/Lead-level scope that sits at the upper edge of Riley's target seniority. Airbnb's consumer marketplace context (two-sided platform with guest + host dynamics) is adjacent but not identical to e-commerce, though the behavioral signals, discovery, and ranking work transfer cleanly.
recommendation_reason: Domain alignment is exceptional — hybrid search, ranking, personalization, recommendations, conversational AI, and A/B experimentation are all proven; the experience gap is minor and the role is squarely in Riley's sweet spot.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving search relevance, personalization, and recommendation systems at scale — including hybrid AI search across 10M+ monthly users at Samsung and ML-powered discovery on a $2B+ GMV e-commerce platform. Proven track record partnering with ML engineers and applied researchers to define model strategy, own end-to-end roadmaps, and ship AI-powered experiences that move bookings, engagement, and satisfaction metrics.
top_bullets: Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing
gap_handling: The 10+ year requirement vs. Riley's 9 years is a soft gap — frame total impact and scope, not just tenure. Emphasize that Samsung's role involves a two-sided dynamic (buyers + sellers / catalog owners), and draw explicit parallels to Airbnb's guest/host marketplace balance. If asked about two-sided platform experience, lean on Amazon's seller-facing work and Samsung's international marketplace launches.

title: Product Manager, Guest Discovery and Homepage
location: San Francisco CA, Los Angeles CA, New York NY
score_rationale: Riley's core DNA — AI-powered discovery, search ranking, personalization, and recommendation systems at consumer scale — maps almost perfectly to Airbnb's homepage and guest discovery mandate. The Samsung.com work (hybrid search, conversational AI, behavioral ranking, 10M+ monthly users) and Kolon's ML-driven recommendation/AOV work are directly relevant proof points. The main gap is that the role appears to sit at a "product leader" level with significant executive stakeholder and marketing narrative responsibilities, and the JD's emphasis on consumer marketplace dynamics (travel/hospitality, multi-vertical inventory) is a domain shift from e-commerce/electronics that Riley hasn't directly worked in.
recommendation_reason: Riley's AI search + discovery + personalization stack is a near-perfect skill match for Airbnb's homepage transformation; strong quantified impact at consumer scale clears the bar to apply confidently.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping AI-powered search, discovery, and personalization experiences at consumer scale — including hybrid search for 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com and ML-driven recommendation systems on a $2B+ GMV platform. Proven track record of building cross-functional alignment with engineering, data science, design, and marketing to deliver measurable growth in engagement, conversion, and retention.
top_bullets: Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation CSAT/NPS by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users.

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, optimizing engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion.

Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing on a $2B+ GMV platform.
gap_handling: The role signals "product leader" expectations — owning executive narratives, cross-vertical roadmap alignment, and marketing storytelling — which is a mild stretch beyond Riley's current PM III scope. In cover materials and interviews, Riley should emphasize the Samsung global expansion approval story (executive alignment), the AEO pipeline that touched marketing channels, and the Samsung.com roadmap ownership to demonstrate readiness for this broader leadership surface area.

title: Senior Product Manager, Shopping Experience
score_rationale: Riley's 9 years of PM experience in e-commerce discovery, personalization, recommendation systems, A/B testing, and consumer-facing search maps very cleanly onto Instacart's Shopping Experience requirements. The Samsung.com and Kolonmall roles provide direct evidence of end-to-end consumer product ownership at scale, data-driven experimentation, and cross-functional leadership. The one meaningful gap is health/nutrition/wellness domain familiarity — this is listed as a preferred (not required) qualifier, so it doesn't torpedo the fit, but the zero-to-one framing of the health area means the hiring bar for domain curiosity will be high.
recommendation_reason: Strong match on consumer e-commerce, discovery, personalization, and experimentation — preferred qualifications are nearly a checklist of Riley's background; health domain gap is manageable.
suggested_summary: Senior Product Manager with 9+ years building AI-powered discovery, personalization, and search experiences on high-scale consumer e-commerce platforms (Samsung.com, 10M+ monthly users; Kolonmall, $2B+ GMV). Proven track record taking zero-to-one bets from ambiguous discovery through scaled launch — driving measurable gains in conversion, engagement, and retention through rigorous A/B experimentation and deep user empathy.
top_bullets: Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing with data scientists, engineers, and UX designers.

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation CSAT (NPS) by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.

Streamlined product discovery funnels by launching category-specific finder experiences and optimizing taxonomy — increasing PDP view rate per visit by 45% (from 30% to 45%) and reducing bounce rate by 50% via improved assortment visibility and guided shopping flows.
gap_handling: The health/nutrition domain is the only meaningful gap. Address it proactively in your cover letter: connect your experience with dietary-filter-style personalization (e.g., size recommendation, preference-driven recommendations at Kolonmall) to health-forward discovery, and signal genuine personal interest in the health/wellness space. You don't need to be a nutritionist — Instacart needs a PM who can navigate ambiguity and build discovery products, which is squarely your wheelhouse.

title: Senior Product Manager, AI (Customer Agent)
score_rationale: Riley's profile is a strong match on AI/ML product experience — conversational AI, LLM pipelines, search ranking, and recommendation systems all transfer well to Klaviyo's Customer Agent role. However, the role is squarely B2B SaaS (brand-facing configuration, onboarding flows, and agent tooling for business customers), while Riley's primary archetypes are AI Search and E-commerce Discovery — the B2B platform exposure at Amazon is real but narrower than what this role demands. Riley also lacks explicit RAG/agent architecture experience stated on the resume, and has no direct messaging-channel PM background (SMS, RCS, email automation), which is central to Klaviyo's multi-channel agent vision.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and conversational product credentials clear the bar, but limited B2B SaaS onboarding and multi-channel messaging experience creates a meaningful gap that requires targeted resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered and conversational AI experiences — including an end-to-end on-site AI chat agent, hybrid search systems, and personalized recommendation pipelines serving 10M+ users. Proven track record collaborating with ML/data science teams to define agent behavior, build feedback loops, and drive measurable outcomes across complex B2C and B2B product surfaces.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users
Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: Riley's resume doesn't explicitly mention RAG, agent architectures, or multi-channel deployment (SMS/RCS/email). Before applying, add a brief callout in the conversational AI bullet noting any retrieval or grounding mechanisms used, and consider adding a line about channel orchestration if the Samsung chat experience touched multiple surfaces. If RAG/LLM infrastructure was involved even peripherally, name it explicitly.

title: Staff Product Manager, Search Algorithms
location: New York City, NY; San Francisco, CA
score_rationale: Riley's core skillset — hybrid AI search, search ranking, relevance metrics, and experimentation — maps very closely to what Faire needs for this Search Algorithms role, and the Samsung.com search work (10M+ monthly users, precision@3 +30%, CTR improvements) provides direct evidence. The main tension is seniority: this is a Staff PM role, which is one level above Riley's target Senior PM tier, and Faire appears to expect someone with deep startup/high-growth product leadership at scale as well as founder-adjacent decision-making. The B2B wholesale/marketplace domain is adjacent but distinct from Riley's B2C e-commerce and Samsung retail experience, meaning domain translation will be needed.
recommendation_reason: Strong search/AI skills and direct evidence match, but the Staff-level seniority and B2B wholesale marketplace context are meaningful stretches requiring deliberate framing.
suggested_summary: AI Search Product Manager with 9+ years shipping high-scale search, ranking, and discovery experiences — including hybrid AI search across 3 international markets for 10M+ monthly users and measurable gains in precision, CTR, and engagement. Experienced working shoulder-to-shoulder with ML/data science teams on complex search systems, defining relevance metrics, and driving experimentation programs that deliver business impact for retailers and sellers at scale.
top_bullets: Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation CSAT score (NPS) by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users
gap_handling: The Staff PM title and Faire's emphasis on "high-growth tech startup" experience is the most notable gap. Riley can address this by explicitly framing Samsung.com as a high-scale commercial platform (not just a corporate site), quantifying the scope of search impact ($B+ revenue surface, 10M+ MAU), and emphasizing the Amazon tenure as a high-growth, ambiguity-heavy startup-style environment. Highlighting direct founder/exec-level alignment moments (e.g., executive approval for global search expansion) also helps close the perceived seniority gap.

title: Product Manager, API Growth
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley is a strong AI/ML PM with deep search and discovery chops, and the AI domain alignment with Anthropic is excellent — but this role is squarely a developer platform / API growth PM role, and Riley's experience is overwhelmingly consumer-facing product work. The 6+ years experience bar and growth funnel expertise (acquisition, activation, monetization via A/B testing) are well-matched, but Riley lacks direct developer tools, API console, or B2B SaaS growth experience. The Amazon B2B listing tool role is the closest signal, but it's seller-facing, not developer-facing. The gap in "developer platform growth" is real and will be a key screen.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI domain and growth funnel credentials, but missing core developer platform / API PM experience that is explicitly required — worth tailoring and applying given Anthropic's visa sponsorship and brand upside.
suggested_summary: Product Manager with 9+ years driving AI-powered growth across acquisition, activation, and monetization funnels — at Samsung.com, Amazon, and Kolon. Proven track record building ML-driven discovery and recommendation systems that scaled to 10M+ monthly users and 2M+ B2B sellers, with deep fluency in LLM products, experimentation, and data-driven iteration that directly translates to Claude Platform growth challenges.
top_bullets: Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines across 20+ categories (Amazon).

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, leading phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels, increasing web navigation CSAT (NPS) by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users.

Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148→$185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing on a $2B+ GMV platform.
gap_handling: The biggest gap is developer platform / API PM experience. To address this: (1) Lean hard into the Amazon role — emphasize that the B2B seller listing tool is essentially a developer-facing self-serve platform with onboarding, console-like workflows, and usage automation. Reframe "sellers" as "technical business users." (2) Highlight the AEO pipeline and conversational AI work at Samsung as evidence of building on top of AI APIs and structured data pipelines. (3) In your cover letter, explicitly acknowledge you are transitioning from consumer/e-commerce growth into developer platform growth, and cite your hands-on LLM experience as the bridge. Do NOT pretend to have developer platform experience you don't have — Anthropic interviewers will probe deeply.

title: Principal, Product Manager - AI / LLM
score_rationale: Riley's AI/LLM product background — conversational AI, RAG-adjacent systems, LLM pipelines (AEO), and search relevance — maps well to this role's core technical asks. The conversational AI chat experience at Samsung and the AEO pipeline directly demonstrate contextual AI and knowledge-layer thinking. However, this is a Principal PM role (above Riley's target seniority of Senior/PM III), it explicitly centers on developer/B2B platform infrastructure (backend services, API orchestration, RAG pipelines for a CPaaS), and Riley's experience skews toward consumer e-commerce discovery rather than developer tooling or internal platform products — creating a meaningful domain and archetype gap.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product credibility and conversational AI delivery experience, but the Principal-level seniority, developer-platform domain, and RAG/backend infrastructure focus are meaningful stretches from Riley's e-commerce discovery background.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years building LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI chat experiences and AEO knowledge pipelines to hybrid search and behavioral personalization — that drive measurable engagement and conversion at scale. Proven track record defining AI product strategy, leading cross-functional delivery with ML/data science teams, and shipping contextual, intelligent product experiences to millions of users.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata (product attributes, inventory, pricing) from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%
gap_handling: The biggest gap is the developer/B2B platform context — Twilio's users are technical builders, not shoppers. In your cover letter and interviews, lean into your Amazon experience (2M+ B2B sellers, ML-driven listing automation, multi-modal pipelines) as your "platform + technical user" proof point, and explicitly connect your AEO pipeline work to RAG-style knowledge retrieval architectures. Be upfront about the seniority stretch by framing yourself as a high-ownership Senior/PM III ready to operate at Principal scope.

title: Staff Product Manager, AI Personalization
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML personalization background, search ranking, LLM product experience, and proven A/B experimentation at scale (Samsung, Amazon, Kolon) map well to the role's technical and personalization requirements. However, this is a Staff-level Platform PM role focused on customer support infrastructure — a domain Riley has no direct experience in — and the "full-stack platform" ownership expectation (RAG, contextual retrieval architecture, prompt optimization at infrastructure level) is a step above Riley's documented experience, which skews toward front-end search/discovery and e-commerce surfaces. The seniority title "Staff PM" is also a stretch above Riley's target (Senior/PM III), and the role appears to require platform-building chops more than consumer discovery chops.
recommendation_reason: Strong personalization and AI/ML credentials meet ~75% of requirements, but Staff-level seniority and customer support platform domain are meaningful gaps that require deliberate tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years building personalization systems, LLM-powered experiences, and search infrastructure at consumer scale — spanning Samsung, Amazon, and Kolon. Proven track record owning end-to-end AI product strategy, from retrieval and ranking model design to consumer-facing launch, with measurable impact across 10M+ monthly users and $2B+ GMV platforms.
top_bullets: Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users
gap_handling: Riley has no direct customer support / CS platform experience — reframe Samsung's conversational AI chat experience and the AEO pipeline as analogous "AI understanding user context and responding accurately at scale" work. Explicitly call out familiarity with RAG/retrieval concepts (even if not in title) and lean on the recommendation API architecture work as evidence of platform-building instincts. Avoid overstating — interviewers at this level will probe depth.

title: Senior Product Manager
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI product experience and stakeholder management credentials that map well to this role's AI-powered Help Center and cross-functional CustOps work. However, the core domain — customer support enablement, help center platforms, knowledge base design, and agent tooling — is not represented in Riley's resume, which is deeply rooted in search/discovery, e-commerce, and recommendation systems. The Growth context partially aligns, but the specific support-surface + deflection-rate focus is a meaningful gap that makes this a "tailor carefully" opportunity rather than a natural fit.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI and growth credentials transfer well, but zero direct support/help-center domain experience is a real gap for a role where that is the core product surface.
suggested_summary: Product leader with 9+ years shipping AI-powered discovery and personalization experiences at scale — including conversational AI, LLM pipelines, and hybrid search for 10M+ monthly users — with a consistent track record of driving measurable growth metrics across the full user lifecycle. Passionate about transforming siloed content surfaces into cohesive, data-driven product experiences that improve user success and accelerate conversion.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation CSAT score (NPS) by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks
gap_handling: Riley has no explicit help center, knowledge base, or customer support tooling experience. To bridge this gap in a cover letter or screen, frame the Samsung conversational AI and search instrumentation work as analogous — both involve surfacing the right content at the right moment to unblock users and reduce friction. Emphasize the NPS/CSAT outcome and the cross-functional CustOps-like stakeholder dynamic at Samsung.com. Acknowledge the domain shift candidly and position it as intentional scope expansion.

title: Staff Product Manager, Lyft AI Platform & Marketplace Applications
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background, search/discovery domain expertise, and hands-on experience with SQL, Tableau, and data-driven decision-making map reasonably well onto this role's technical requirements. However, this is explicitly a Staff PM role at an AI *platform* and *marketplace* company (ride-sharing, not e-commerce), requiring deep agentic AI / multi-agent orchestration experience and a platform/internal-tooling archetype — neither of which is a primary strength in Riley's portfolio. The 7+ year requirement is met (9 years), but the stretch to Staff-level and the platform-vs-product PM gap are real hurdles.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and data credentials meet core bar, but the Staff-level stretch, platform PM archetype mismatch, and ride-sharing domain distance make this competitive — apply only with heavy tailoring toward agentic AI and internal platform work.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-driven pipelines, hybrid search systems, and ML-powered platforms at scale for 10M+ users — including an end-to-end conversational AI product and an automated AEO metadata pipeline that drove 8x AI agent traffic. Skilled at bridging technical depth and business impact, leading cross-functional teams of engineers and scientists, and evangelizing AI-powered products to executive stakeholders.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience — from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch — in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users
Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%
gap_handling: The biggest gap is platform PM experience — this role is about building and evangelizing an *internal AI platform* consumed by other Lyft teams, not shipping end-user products. Riley should surface the AEO pipeline (infrastructure-like, adopted across channels), the recommendation API (developer-facing), and the Amazon B2B listing automation (ML pipeline + cross-team enablement) as the closest proxies. Explicitly calling out experience enabling internal stakeholder adoption and defining platform KPIs will help bridge this gap.

title: Product Manager, Consumer
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials, a proven track record shipping search, discovery, and conversational AI at scale, and 9 years of PM experience that comfortably clears the 8-year bar. However, the role explicitly calls for a "former consumer founder" who spikes on 0-to-1 product creation — Riley's background is primarily 1-to-N optimization and feature scaling at established companies (Samsung, Amazon, Kolon), not greenfield consumer product invention or startup founding. The hybrid SF/NYC in-office requirement (25%+) is manageable from Bellevue but adds friction, and the $385K–$460K comp signals this is a very senior, high-bar hire where the founder-pedigree filter is real.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/consumer domain fit and solid credentials, but the "former founder" and 0-to-1 product invention requirement is a real gap that makes this a competitive stretch without startup experience.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping consumer-facing AI experiences — including conversational AI chat, hybrid search, and personalized discovery systems serving 10M+ monthly users — with a track record of translating emerging model capabilities into products people love. Deeply hands-on across the full product lifecycle from vision and prototyping through scaled global launch, with a bias for creative, low-cost experimentation to validate product-market fit fast.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch, supporting product discovery and comparison for millions of users on Samsung.com

Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks — a 0-to-1 initiative taken from concept to production

Launched hybrid AI-powered search experience across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion
gap_handling: Riley has no stated startup founding experience — the single biggest filter for this role. To address this, the cover letter should reframe the AEO pipeline and conversational AI chat launches as 0-to-1 entrepreneurial bets driven by Riley's initiative, not top-down roadmap items. If Riley has any side projects, independent AI product experiments, or early-stage advisory work, those should be surfaced prominently. Leaning into hands-on prototyping with Claude or other AI tools (per the JD's "Claude Code" signal) would also help close the founder-proxy gap.

title: Product Manager, Monetization
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/Search/E-commerce credentials and 9 years of PM experience that clears the 6-year bar, but this role is fundamentally a Growth/Monetization PM role — pricing, packaging, subscription funnels, and free-to-paid conversion — which is only a partial match to Riley's core archetype of AI Search and Discovery. Riley has relevant signals (CRM, onboarding conversion, AOV optimization, retention at Kolon; seller cost savings at Amazon) but lacks direct, sustained ownership of monetization/pricing systems at scale. The domain is Consumer AI at Anthropic, which aligns well with Riley's AI-first background, but the PM archetype mismatch (Growth/Monetization vs. Search/Discovery) is the central gap.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI brand and location fit, but the core Growth/Monetization PM archetype is a meaningful gap versus Riley's Search & Discovery specialty — apply with heavy tailoring toward conversion and funnel work.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving measurable revenue and engagement growth across AI-powered consumer and e-commerce products at Samsung, Amazon, and Kolon. Proven track record in funnel optimization, A/B testing, personalization, and conversion — with hands-on experience shipping AI-native experiences to 10M+ users at scale.
top_bullets: Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing

Restructured new customer benefit package and revamped onboarding workflow, increasing first purchase rate by 30%. Designed segmentation-driven CRM marketing program and boosted 1-year retention by 7% and re-purchase rate by 15%
gap_handling: Riley does not have an explicit "monetization PM" title or direct ownership of pricing/packaging/subscription tier design. To bridge this gap: (1) reframe the Kolon onboarding + retention work as a conversion/growth story with revenue impact; (2) call out the AOV and conversion rate improvements as funnel monetization evidence; (3) in the cover letter, explicitly connect AEO pipeline (+40% conversion) and recommendation API (AOV +15%) to revenue growth strategy, not just feature delivery.

title: Senior Product Manager - Data Agent
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product chops, SQL fluency, LLM pipeline experience (AEO, conversational AI), and strong experimentation background translate well to the AI-powered query experience Datadog is building. However, the role has a pronounced B2B SaaS / enterprise data stack core — BI tools, semantic layers, data warehouses, observability — that Riley has not worked in directly; her domain is e-commerce search and discovery, not analytics infrastructure. The 0-to-1 entrepreneurial framing and the governance/BI competitive landscape (Looker, dbt, Snowflake, etc.) represent a real gap that will be apparent in interviews.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM and data product signals, but the enterprise BI / modern data stack domain is a genuine stretch that requires deliberate resume tailoring and interview prep.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered pipelines, conversational AI experiences, and data-driven discovery systems at Samsung and Amazon. Proven track record owning 0-to-1 AI products end-to-end — from eval frameworks and ranking systems to governance-ready data pipelines — with strong SQL and analytics fluency to drive decisions in ambiguous, fast-moving environments.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata (product attributes, inventory, pricing) from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%; partnered with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines across 20+ high-impact categories
gap_handling: Address the BI/data stack gap head-on: study the modern data stack (dbt, Snowflake/BigQuery semantic layer, Looker/Metabase, NL-to-SQL players like ThoughtSpot and Databricks Genie) before applying. In your cover letter and interviews, draw direct parallels — your AEO metadata pipeline ≈ semantic/context layer; your search relevance eval framework ≈ LLM eval/confidence scoring; your CDP integration work ≈ unified data layer design. This reframing is credible but must be explicit.

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
location: Bellevue, Washington; Chicago, Illinois; New York, New York; Washington, DC
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — shipping LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and search systems — which covers the "AI-adjacent" half of this role well. However, the core requirement is deep Identity & Access Management expertise (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust, agent authentication/authorization), a domain Riley has no evident background in. This is a developer/security-platform PM role, not a consumer AI or e-commerce discovery role, so the archetype fit is meaningful but partial. The AEO pipeline and API design experience (recommendation API, structured data) hint at technical depth, but IAM is a hard, specialized requirement that can't easily be papered over.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product credibility covers ~half the role, but zero IAM/OAuth/OIDC/security experience is a significant gap for the core requirement of this position.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI and hybrid search to structured data pipelines and recommendation APIs — at Samsung and Amazon scale. Experienced in defining technical protocols, partnering with engineers on system design, and launching "zero-to-one" AI products for millions of users; eager to apply this foundation to securing the next generation of AI agent identities at Okta.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%
gap_handling: Be upfront in your cover letter: acknowledge you're coming from the AI product side rather than the IAM/security side, and position yourself as the "AI-native PM who understands agentic systems from the inside." Reference your multi-agent workflow familiarity and API design experience explicitly. Consider quickly studying OAuth2/OIDC flows and Zero Trust basics (Auth0 docs, Okta developer blog) so you can speak credibly to the protocol layer in an interview.
